mechanism_narrative: A sustained low effective federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day S&P 500 returns following months with below-median FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 6.0% signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers institutional de-risking and forced equity liquidations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad demand and supply collapse that overwhelms sectoral diversification, generating strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from INDPRO and RSAFS yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not significantly improve return prediction relative to the single best proxy (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary regimes, a decline in industrial production reflects policy-induced supply contraction rather than temporary disruption, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, month-over-month declines in INDPRO fail to coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0%, liquidity trap expectations dominate discount rate effects, causing investors to discount deeper recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment and falling retail sales produce a nonlinear household income-consumption contraction signal that accelerates equity risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-RSAFS interaction does not significantly predict 5-day S&P 500 returns (regression t-stat p > 0.05) or the interaction-driven Sharpe ratio underperforms the equal-weighted single-signal Sharpe by more than 0.2 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated CPI level above 200 erodes real corporate earnings expectations and tightens household purchasing power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 45% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in housing starts signals recovering residential investment and durable goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of month-over-month HOUST growth do not produce significantly positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
